summary: A high‑energy, soundboard recording that captures an incendiary Playing in the Band – superb performance despite a slightly hissy mix.
review: The second‑set capture is a soundboard source with good overall clarity; listeners note some hiss and occasional over‑loud bass but praise the rich mix. Musically this set is legendary, highlighted by what many call the best Playing in the Band of ’72, with the band “on fire” and Phil’s bass soaring to ecstatic heights.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is ~4.3/5; overwhelming praise for the performance (especially the PITB) outweighs minor sound‑quality complaints, yielding a strong combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Rich SBD mix with occasional hiss and slightly over‑blown loudness; Phil’s bass can dominate the mix, but overall the recording is clear enough to enjoy the intense performance.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Only the second set is available; it builds into a furious, high‑intensity climax centered on Playing in the Band.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as “trey‑ing out”, twangy vocals and wild guitar work during the jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Repeatedly called “on fire”, bass massive, sometimes too loud or over‑mixed but a major driver of the intensity.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann praised as an underrated drummer; his playing noted as solid and supportive.
